title: Contextual actions menu for sidebar items (replace always-visible icon row)
description: Per `plano-de-interface.md`'s `# Ações` section ('Evitar botões espalhados. Priorizar ações contextuais.' with the example 'Página ⋮ Renomear / Duplicar / Mover / Excluir'), consolidate `SidebarTree.tsx`'s `NodeRow` hover icon row (rename/move-up/move-down/delete as 4 separate buttons, from TASK-78) into a single '⋮' trigger that opens a small menu. This keeps the sidebar visually calm per the plan's 'evitar excesso de botões' guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: Each section/page row in the sidebar shows a single '⋮' affordance on hover/focus (replacing the current 4 separate icon buttons), opening a shadcn `DropdownMenu`/`Popover` with: Renomear, Mover para cima, Mover para baixo, Excluir — using the exact same mutations already wired in `SidebarTree.tsx` (`rename`, `reorder` via `move`, `remove`)

'Duplicar' and cross-section 'Mover' from the plan's example are explicitly out of scope (no backend support exists for either today) — they are omitted from the menu rather than added as dead/disabled options, and the gap is noted in technicalNotes for potential future tasks

Rename still uses the existing inline-edit pattern (input replaces the row) triggered from the menu's 'Renomear' item, rather than a separate modal — reuse `NodeRow`'s existing `editing` state machinery
Move up/down items are hidden/disabled at the first/last position exactly as today's `invisible` class behavior on the icon buttons
Delete keeps the existing `window.confirm` guard and cascade-delete warning copy, unchanged

Keyboard accessibility: the ⋮ trigger and menu items are reachable and operable via keyboard (shadcn `DropdownMenu` provides this by default) — this also closes part of the plan's 'discreet but always available' structure principle

steps.description: Replace `NodeRow`'s action buttons with a single trigger
steps.details: Swap the 4-button `<span>` group in `NodeRow` (SidebarTree.tsx) for a `DropdownMenuTrigger` styled as a single '⋮' icon button, visible on row hover/focus exactly like today's group.

`DropdownMenuContent` with Renomear/Mover para cima/Mover para baixo/Excluir items calling the same callback props `NodeRow` already receives (`onRename` trigger via `editing` state, `onMoveUp`, `onMoveDown`, `onDelete`).

steps.description: Verify across all three current row types
steps.details: Section rows, page rows (post-TASK-86, tabs no longer nested here) — confirm the menu behaves identically to the old buttons for create/rename/reorder/delete, including the first/last-position disabling and the delete confirmation copy.

technicalNotes: Depends on TASK-86 landing first since that task removes the tab-nesting layer this component also renders through — avoid conflicting rewrites of `SidebarTree.tsx` in parallel.

'Duplicar' (page/section) and cross-section 'Mover' have no backend mutation today (only reorder-within-parent exists). Do not add UI for capabilities the API can't perform; this is a known gap vs. the plan's literal example, not an oversight.
Reuses shadcn `DropdownMenu` — check it's already installed from TASK-80 (used for management screens) before adding a new dependency.

TASK-85 (header) may have already built a minimal per-menu ⋮ control for menu rename/reorder/delete, ahead of this task in sequence. If so, extract a shared `RowActionsMenu` component here instead of maintaining two near-identical dropdown implementations.
